abstract.text: Normalizes the project’s files by removing trailing whitespace, applying Unix newlines, performing canonical decomposition and formatting Swift files.

inlineContent.text: The general formatting is done by applying Swift’s official formatter, known as swift‐format. More information about it is available at ‹https://github.com/apple/swift-format›.

Inside a Workspace configuration, the configuration for swift‐format is found under “proofreading.swiftFormatConfiguration” and it uses the Swift API from its own SwiftFormatConfiguration module. See ‹https://github.com/apple/swift-format/blob/508.0.0/Sources/SwiftFormatConfiguration/Configuration.swift#L20›
Setting the entire property to nil will completely disable swift‐format.
: A language to use instead of the one specified in preferences.
: The location of the target project if it is not at the current working directory.
: Attempts to download and temporarily use the specified version insead of the one which is installed.

: A list of IETF language tags or SDGCornerstone language icons. Semicolons indicate fallback order. Commas indicate that multiple languages should be used. Examples: “en-US” or “🇺🇸EN” → American English, “nv,en;es” → both Navajo and English, otherwise Spanish
: A file system path. The form “/…” indicates an absolute path. The form “~/…” indicates a path relative to the home directory. Anything else is interpreted relative to the current working directory.

abstract.text: Workspace automates management of Swift projects.
